revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | Name = Kano Shrine and Kano Gozen Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Kano-jinja (Naiku) 01.jpg | location = Kano, {{ill|Tamaki|en|Tamaki|WD=Q1289593|lt=Tamaki|1=Tamaki}}, {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|WD=Q1131032|lt=Watarai District|1=Watarai District}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34.489417|N|136.600611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Kano Shrine: Ōkami no Mikagegawa no Kami
Kano Gozen Shrine: Gozen no Kami | type = Kano Shrine: {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor Rank), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Tradition holds during Emperor Yūryaku's reign | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = Kanamori-san | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) | location = {{ill|Tamaki|WD=Q587348|1=Tamaki, Mie}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }} '''Kano Shrine''' (蚊野神社, *Kano Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the [[Kōtai Jingū]] (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in {{ill|Tamaki|en|Tamaki|WD=Q1289593|lt=Tamaki|1=Tamaki}}, {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|WD=Q1131032|lt=Watarai District|1=Watarai District}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan. It holds the 7th Rank among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall, ed. (1929): pp. 9–11. This article also covers '''Kano Gozen Shrine''' (蚊野御前神社, *Kano Gozen Jinja*), which is enshrined alongside Kano Shrine. Both shrines are dedicated to deities protecting the tributaries of the Sotokida River.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 68. == Overview == This section covers information common to both shrines. Located at 1807 Kanosatonaka, {{ill|Tamaki, Mie|en|Tamaki, Mie|WD=Q587348|lt=Tamaki, Mie|1=Tamaki, Mie}}, [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]], {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 60 The area of Kano, where the shrines are situated, lies within a rural landscape, with {{ill|Kano Shrine|ja|蚊野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kano Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Kano Shrine|ja|蚊野神社|lt_ja=蚊野御前神社|lt=Kano Gozen Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kano Shrine|qq=}} enshrined within a large forest in the local settlement. The shrine grounds cover an area of 8,383 m².Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 196 In ancient times, the shrine grounds spanned approximately 2 chō (≈19,834 m²).Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150 The precincts are covered with moss.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 64 The shrine buildings are constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} style with board roofing, facing south. A single-layered {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|WD=Q3078312|lt=tamagaki|1=Sacred fences}} fence with a gate surrounds the shrine, and a single {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|WD=Q234731|lt=Shinmei torii|1=Torii}} stands at the entrance. == Kano Shrine == Kano Shrine is an auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine of Ise and enshrines a {{ill|Mirror|en|Mirror|WD=Q35197|lt=mirror|1=Mirror}} as its sacred object.Kaneko (1983): p. 59 It is affectionately known as "Kanamori-san." In ''Daijingū Gishiki Kai,'' Nakagawa Tsunemasa explains that "Kanamori" is a corruption of "Kano Mori," and the shrine’s name derives from the name of the area where it is located.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 195 The enshrined deity is Ōkami Mikagekawa no Kami (大神御蔭川神). This deity is considered the god of a tributary of the Sotoda River, which flows through the Kano area, and is revered as a local development god. The same deity is enshrined at {{ill|Mifune Shrine|ja|御船神社 (多気町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mifune Shrine|qq=}}, another auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine. The ''Jinmyōchō Kōshō'' identifies the deity as {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsuhime|ja|瀬織津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 瀬織津姫}} and interprets the name Ōkami Mikagekawa no Kami as "the spirit of {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}, purifying sins by the river." In ancient times, the shrine hall was specified to be 10 shaku (≈3.03 m) in length, 9 shaku (≈2.73 m) in width, and 5 shaku (≈1.52 m) in height, with an altar measuring 4.5 shaku (≈1.36 m) by 2.5 shaku (≈0.758 m) and 1 shaku (≈0.303 m) in height. The shrine was surrounded by a sacred fence (mizugaki), gate, and tamagaki. == Kano Gozen Shrine == Kano Gozen Shrine is Ranked 8th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine, following Kano Shrine. The main hall has been discontinued, and the deity is now enshrined together with Kano Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10 The enshrined deity is Gozen no Kami (御前神). The ''History of Ujiyamada City'' refers to this deity as "Kano Gozen no Kami." Similar to the deity of Kano Shrine, this deity is considered the god of a tributary of the Sotoda River. Gozen no Kami is regarded as the deity who calms and supports Ōkami Mikagekawa no Kami. In ancient times, the shrine had an independent hall measuring 3.5 shaku (≈1.06 m) in length, 2.4 shaku (≈0.727 m) in width, and 7 shaku (≈2.12 m) in height. == History == According to the ''Taijingū Shiki,'' the shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}. It was designated as one of the shrines to be rebuilt every 20 years alongside the main shrine, with officials assigned to oversee the reconstruction. During the {{ill|Middle Ages|en|Middle Ages|WD=Q12554|lt=medieval period|1=Middle Ages}}, the rebuilding process became irregular. For example, during the reconstruction on the 30th day of the 12th month in {{ill|Bun'ei|en|Bun'ei|WD=Q1010103|lt=Bunei|1=Bun'ei}} 3 (Julian calendar: January 26, 1267), the Gozen Shrine, its gate, sacred fence, and torii were not rebuilt. The last clear record of reconstruction was on the 8th day of the 12th month in {{ill|Ryakuō|en|Ryakuō|WD=Q1146182|lt=Ryakuō|1=Ryakuō}} 3 (Julian calendar: December 27, 1340), when materials from {{ill|Kaga Province|en|Kaga Province|WD=Q907469|lt=Kaga Province|1=Kaga Province}} were ordered for the rebuilding. On September 7, {{ill|Kanbun (era)|en|Kanbun (era)|WD=Q1146228|lt=Kanbun|1=Kanbun (era)}} 3 (Gregorian calendar: October 7, 1663), thanks to the efforts of the Chief Priest {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was restored at its current location. A site survey before the restoration confirmed the presence of post holes and decayed pillars, identifying the site as the old shrine grounds. There were no objections to the decision to restore the shrine at this location. The shrine buildings were rebuilt in October {{ill|1916|en|1916|WD=Q2087|lt=1916|1=1916}} (Taishō 5). == Rituals == During the {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai|1=Kinensai}}, {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (in June and December), {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai|1=Kannamesai Festival}}, {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}, and temporary festivals, rituals are performed by priests in front of the shrine. For the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}}, {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}}, National Foundation Day Festival, {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天皇誕生日|1=The Emperor's Birthday|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q72318}}, and Wind Day Prayer Festivals (in May and August), remote rituals are conducted at the Gojōden of the Inner Shrine. == Surroundings == The {{ill|Kumano Saigoku Kaidō|ja|熊野西国街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Saigoku Kaidō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} passes nearby, with remnants of pine tree-lined paths still visible. Additionally, many ancient Kofuns have been discovered in this area, including 19 kofun in the Bōzu-yama Kofun group. * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Tokida Station|en|Tokida Station|WD=Q7813584|lt=Sotoda Station|1=Tokida Station}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|en|Sangū Line|WD=Q872023|lt=Sangū Line|1=Sangū Line}} operated by {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|en|Central Japan Railway Company|WD=Q513679|lt=JR Central|1=Central Japan Railway Company}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』{{ill|Kogakkan University|en|Kogakkan University|WD=Q1797584|lt=Kogakkan University|1=Kogakkan University}}出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[:en:小学館|小学館]]、昭和50th year 4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[東外城田村]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_01.jpg 蚊野神社] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image2 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_02.jpg 蚊野御前神社]}} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0706.html 蚊野神社（かのじんじゃ） 御同座 蚊野御前神社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|蚊野神社|version=77001993|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Kano Shrine (Q17225745)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|ceb|Watarai-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی واتاری|es|Distrito de Watarai|fr|District de Watarai|id|Distrik Watarai, Mie|it|Distretto di Watarai|ko|와타라이군|pl|Powiat Watarai|uk|Повіт Ватарай|zh|度會郡|lt=Watarai district|WD=Q1131032|1=Watarai District}} || {{ill|Kano Shrine|lt=Kano Shrine|ja|蚊野神社|WD=Q17225745|1=Kano Shrine}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} || part of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|WD=Q11581011|1=Kōtai Jingū}} || — || {{coord|34.489433|136.600652|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181116 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17225745}}==Notes== [[da:d:Q17225745]] [[ja:蚊野神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Buildings in Tamaki Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha merges]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tamaki]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

(1975): p. 150 The precincts are covered with moss.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 64 The shrine buildings are constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} style with board roofing, facing south. A single-layered {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|WD=Q3078312|lt=tamagaki|1=Sacred fences}} fence with a gate surrounds the shrine, and a single {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|WD=Q234731|lt=Shinmei torii|1=Torii}} stands at the entrance. == Kano Shrine == Kano Shrine is an auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine of Ise and enshrines a {{ill|Mirror|en|Mirror|WD=Q35197|lt=mirror|1=Mirror}} as its sacred object.Kaneko (1983): p. 59 It is affectionately known as "Kanamori-san." In ''Daijingū Gishiki Kai,'' Nakagawa Tsunemasa explains that "Kanamori" is a corruption of "Kano Mori," and the shrine’s name derives from the name of the area where it is located.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 195 The enshrined deity is Ōkami Mikagekawa no Kami (大神御蔭川神). This deity is considered the god of a tributary of the Sotoda River, which flows through the Kano area, and is revered as a local development god. The ''Jinmyōchō Kōshō'' identifies the deity as {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsuhime|ja|瀬織津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 瀬織津姫}} and interprets the name Ōkami Mikagekawa no Kami as "the spirit of {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}, purifying sins by the river." In ancient times, the shrine hall was specified to be 10 shaku (≈3.03 m) in length, 9 shaku (≈2.73 m) in width, and 5 shaku (≈1.52 m) in height, with an altar measuring 4.5 shaku (≈1.36 m) by 2.5 shaku (≈0.758 m) and 1 shaku (≈0.303 m) in height. The shrine was surrounded by a sacred fence (mizugaki), gate, and tamagaki. == Kano Gozen Shrine == Kano Gozen Shrine is Ranked 8th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine, following Kano Shrine. The main hall has been discontinued, and the deity is now enshrined together with Kano Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10 The enshrined deity is Gozen no Kami (御前神). The ''History of Ujiyamada City'' refers to this deity as "Kano Gozen no Kami." Similar to the deity of Kano Shrine, this deity is considered the god of a tributary of the Sotoda River. Gozen no Kami is regarded as the deity who calms and supports Ōkami Mikagekawa no Kami. In ancient times, the shrine had an independent hall measuring 3.5 shaku (≈1.06 m) in length, 2.4 shaku (≈0.727 m) in width, and 7 shaku (≈2.12 m) in height. == History == According to the ''Taijingū Shiki,'' the shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}. It was designated as one of the shrines to be rebuilt every 20 years alongside the main shrine, with officials assigned to oversee the reconstruction. During the {{ill|Middle Ages|en|Middle Ages|WD=Q12554|lt=medieval period|1=Middle Ages}}, the rebuilding process became irregular. For example, during the reconstruction on the 30th day of the 12th month in {{ill|Bun'ei|en|Bun'ei|WD=Q1010103|lt=Bunei|1=Bun'ei}} 3 (Julian calendar: January 26, 1267), the Gozen Shrine, its gate, sacred fence, and torii were not rebuilt. The last clear record of reconstruction was on the 8th day of the 12th month in {{ill|Ryakuō|en|Ryakuō|WD=Q1146182|lt=Ryakuō|1=Ryakuō}} 3 (Julian calendar: December 27, 1340), when materials from {{ill|Kaga Province|en|Kaga Province|WD=Q907469|lt=Kaga Province|1=Kaga Province}} were ordered for the rebuilding. On September 7, {{ill|Kanbun (era)|en|Kanbun (era)|WD=Q1146228|lt=Kanbun|1=Kanbun (era)}} 3 (Gregorian calendar: October 7, 1663), thanks to the efforts of the Chief Priest {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was restored at its current location. A site survey before the restoration confirmed the presence of post holes and decayed pillars, identifying the site as the old shrine grounds. There were no objections to the decision to restore the shrine at this location. The shrine buildings were rebuilt in October {{ill|1916|en|1916|WD=Q2087|lt=1916|1=1916}} (Taishō 5). == Rituals == During the {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai|1=Kinensai}}, {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (in June and December), {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai|1=Kannamesai Festival}}, {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}, and temporary festivals, rituals are performed by priests in front of the shrine. For the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}}, {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}}, National Foundation Day Festival, {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天皇誕生日|1=The Emperor's Birthday|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q72318}}, and Wind Day Prayer Festivals (in May and August), remote rituals are conducted at the Gojōden of the Inner Shrine. == Surroundings == The {{ill|Kumano Saigoku Kaidō|ja|熊野西国街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Saigoku Kaidō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} passes nearby, with remnants of pine tree-lined paths still visible. Additionally, many ancient Kofuns have been discovered in this area, including 19 kofun in the Bōzu-yama Kofun group. * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Tokida Station|en|Tokida Station|WD=Q7813584|lt=Sotoda Station|1=Tokida Station}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|en|Sangū Line|WD=Q872023|lt=Sangū Line|1=Sangū Line}} operated by {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|en|Central Japan Railway Company|WD=Q513679|lt=JR Central|1=Central Japan Railway Company}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』{{ill|Kogakkan University|en|Kogakkan University|WD=Q1797584|lt=Kogakkan University|1=Kogakkan University}}出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[:en:小学館|小学館]]、昭和50th year 4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[東外城田村]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_01.jpg 蚊野神社] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image2 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_02.jpg 蚊野御前神社]}} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0706.html 蚊野神社（かのじんじゃ） 御同座 蚊野御前神社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|蚊野神社|version=77001993|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Kano Shrine (Q17225745)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|ceb|Watarai-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی واتاری|es|Distrito de Watarai|fr|District de Watarai|id|Distrik Watarai, Mie|it|Distretto di Watarai|ko|와타라이군|pl|Powiat Watarai|uk|Повіт Ватарай|zh|度會郡|lt=Watarai district|WD=Q1131032|1=Watarai District}} || {{ill|Kano Shrine|lt=Kano Shrine|ja|蚊野神社|WD=Q17225745|1=Kano Shrine}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} || part of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|WD=Q11581011|1=Kōtai Jingū}} || — || {{coord|34.489433|136.600652|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181116 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17225745}}==Notes== [[da:d:Q17225745]] [[ja:蚊野神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Buildings in Tamaki Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tamaki]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

{{Infobox religious building | Name = Kano Shrine and Kano Gozen Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Kano-jinja (Naiku) 01.jpg | location = Kano, {{ill|Tamaki|en|Tamaki|WD=Q1289593|lt=Tamaki|1=Tamaki}}, {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|WD=Q1131032|lt=Watarai District|1=Watarai District}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34.489417|N|136.600611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Kano Shrine: Ōkami no Mikagegawa no Kami
Kano Gozen Shrine: Gozen no Kami | type = Kano Shrine: {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor Rank), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Tradition holds during Emperor Yūryaku's reign | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = Kanamori-san | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) | location = {{ill|Tamaki|WD=Q587348|1=Tamaki, Mie}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }} '''Kano Shrine''' (蚊野神社, *Kano Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the [[Kōtai Jingū]] (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in {{ill|Tamaki|en|Tamaki|WD=Q1289593|lt=Tamaki|1=Tamaki}}, {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|WD=Q1131032|lt=Watarai District|1=Watarai District}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan. It holds the 7th Rank among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall, ed. (1929): pp. 9–11. This article also covers '''Kano Gozen Shrine''' (蚊野御前神社, *Kano Gozen Jinja*), which is enshrined alongside Kano Shrine. Both shrines are dedicated to deities protecting the tributaries of the Sotokida River.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 68. == Overview == This section covers information common to both shrines. Located at 1807 Kanosatonaka, {{ill|Tamaki, Mie|en|Tamaki, Mie|WD=Q587348|lt=Tamaki, Mie|1=Tamaki, Mie}}, [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]], {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 60 The area of Kano, where the shrines are situated, lies within a rural landscape, with {{ill|Kano Shrine|ja|蚊野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kano Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Kano Shrine|ja|蚊野神社|lt_ja=蚊野御前神社|lt=Kano Gozen Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kano Shrine|qq=}} enshrined within a large forest in the local settlement. The shrine grounds cover an area of 8,383 m².Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 196 In ancient times, the shrine grounds spanned approximately 2 chō (≈19,834 m²).Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150 The precincts are covered with moss.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 64 The shrine buildings are constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} style with board roofing, facing south. A single-layered {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|WD=Q3078312|lt=tamagaki|1=Sacred fences}} fence with a gate surrounds the shrine, and a single {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|WD=Q234731|lt=Shinmei torii|1=Torii}} stands at the entrance. == Kano Shrine == Kano Shrine is an auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine of Ise and enshrines a {{ill|Mirror|en|Mirror|WD=Q35197|lt=mirror|1=Mirror}} as its sacred object.Kaneko (1983): p. 59 It is affectionately known as "Kanamori-san." In ''Daijingū Gishiki Kai,'' Nakagawa Tsunemasa explains that "Kanamori" is a corruption of "Kano Mori," and the shrine’s name derives from the name of the area where it is located.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 195 The enshrined deity is Ōkami Mikagekawa no Kami (大神御蔭川神). This deity is considered the god of a tributary of the Sotoda River, which flows through the Kano area, and is revered as a local development god. The same deity is enshrined at {{ill|Mifune Shrine|ja|御船神社 (多気町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mifune Shrine|qq=}}, another auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine. The ''Jinmyōchō Kōshō'' identifies the deity as {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsuhime|ja|瀬織津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 瀬織津姫}} and interprets the name Ōkami Mikagekawa no Kami as "the spirit of {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}, purifying sins by the river." In ancient times, the shrine hall was specified to be 10 shaku (≈3.03 m) in length, 9 shaku (≈2.73 m) in width, and 5 shaku (≈1.52 m) in height, with an altar measuring 4.5 shaku (≈1.36 m) by 2.5 shaku (≈0.758 m) and 1 shaku (≈0.303 m) in height. The shrine was surrounded by a sacred fence (mizugaki), gate, and tamagaki. == Kano Gozen Shrine == Kano Gozen Shrine is Ranked 8th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine, following Kano Shrine. The main hall has been discontinued, and the deity is now enshrined together with Kano Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10 The enshrined deity is Gozen no Kami (御前神). The ''History of Ujiyamada City'' refers to this deity as "Kano Gozen no Kami." Similar to the deity of Kano Shrine, this deity is considered the god of a tributary of the Sotoda River. Gozen no Kami is regarded as the deity who calms and supports Ōkami Mikagekawa no Kami. In ancient times, the shrine had an independent hall measuring 3.5 shaku (≈1.06 m) in length, 2.4 shaku (≈0.727 m) in width, and 7 shaku (≈2.12 m) in height. == History == According to the ''Taijingū Shiki,'' the shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}. It was designated as one of the shrines to be rebuilt every 20 years alongside the main shrine, with officials assigned to oversee the reconstruction. During the {{ill|Middle Ages|en|Middle Ages|WD=Q12554|lt=medieval period|1=Middle Ages}}, the rebuilding process became irregular. For example, during the reconstruction on the 30th day of the 12th month in {{ill|Bun'ei|en|Bun'ei|WD=Q1010103|lt=Bunei|1=Bun'ei}} 3 (Julian calendar: January 26, 1267), the Gozen Shrine, its gate, sacred fence, and torii were not rebuilt. The last clear record of reconstruction was on the 8th day of the 12th month in {{ill|Ryakuō|en|Ryakuō|WD=Q1146182|lt=Ryakuō|1=Ryakuō}} 3 (Julian calendar: December 27, 1340), when materials from {{ill|Kaga Province|en|Kaga Province|WD=Q907469|lt=Kaga Province|1=Kaga Province}} were ordered for the rebuilding. On September 7, {{ill|Kanbun (era)|en|Kanbun (era)|WD=Q1146228|lt=Kanbun|1=Kanbun (era)}} 3 (Gregorian calendar: October 7, 1663), thanks to the efforts of the Chief Priest {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was restored at its current location. A site survey before the restoration confirmed the presence of post holes and decayed pillars, identifying the site as the old shrine grounds. There were no objections to the decision to restore the shrine at this location. The shrine buildings were rebuilt in October {{ill|1916|en|1916|WD=Q2087|lt=1916|1=1916}} (Taishō 5). == Rituals == During the {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai|1=Kinensai}}, {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (in June and December), {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai|1=Kannamesai Festival}}, {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}, and temporary festivals, rituals are performed by priests in front of the shrine. For the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}}, {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}}, National Foundation Day Festival, {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天皇誕生日|1=The Emperor's Birthday|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q72318}}, and Wind Day Prayer Festivals (in May and August), remote rituals are conducted at the Gojōden of the Inner Shrine. == Surroundings == The {{ill|Kumano Saigoku Kaidō|ja|熊野西国街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Saigoku Kaidō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} passes nearby, with remnants of pine tree-lined paths still visible. Additionally, many ancient Kofuns have been discovered in this area, including 19 kofun in the Bōzu-yama Kofun group. * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Tokida Station|en|Tokida Station|WD=Q7813584|lt=Sotoda Station|1=Tokida Station}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|en|Sangū Line|WD=Q872023|lt=Sangū Line|1=Sangū Line}} operated by {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|en|Central Japan Railway Company|WD=Q513679|lt=JR Central|1=Central Japan Railway Company}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[東外城田村]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_01.jpg 蚊野神社] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image2 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_02.jpg 蚊野御前神社]}} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0706.html 蚊野神社（かのじんじゃ） 御同座 蚊野御前神社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|蚊野神社|version=77001993|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Kano Shrine (Q17225745)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | Name = Kano Shrine and Kano Gozen Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Kano-jinja (Naiku) 01.jpg | location = Kano, {{ill|Tamaki|en|Tamaki|WD=Q1289593|lt=Tamaki|1=Tamaki}}, {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|WD=Q1131032|lt=Watarai District|1=Watarai District}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34.489417|N|136.600611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Kano Shrine: Ōkami no Mikagegawa no Kami
Kano Gozen Shrine: Gozen no Kami | type = Kano Shrine: {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor Rank), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Tradition holds during Emperor Yūryaku's reign | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = Kanamori-san | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) | location = {{ill|Tamaki|WD=Q587348|1=Tamaki, Mie}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }} '''Kano Shrine''' (蚊野神社, *Kano Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the [[Kōtai Jingū]] (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in {{ill|Tamaki|en|Tamaki|WD=Q1289593|lt=Tamaki|1=Tamaki}}, {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|WD=Q1131032|lt=Watarai District|1=Watarai District}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan. It holds the 7th Rank among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall, ed. (1929): pp. 9–11. This article also covers '''Kano Gozen Shrine''' (蚊野御前神社, *Kano Gozen Jinja*), which is enshrined alongside Kano Shrine. Both shrines are dedicated to deities protecting the tributaries of the Sotokida River.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 68. == Overview == This section covers information common to both shrines. Located at 1807 Kanosatonaka, {{ill|Tamaki, Mie|en|Tamaki, Mie|WD=Q587348|lt=Tamaki, Mie|1=Tamaki, Mie}}, [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]], {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 60 The area of Kano, where the shrines are situated, lies within a rural landscape, with {{ill|Kano Shrine|ja|蚊野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kano Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Kano Shrine|ja|蚊野神社|lt_ja=蚊野御前神社|lt=Kano Gozen Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kano Shrine|qq=}} enshrined within a large forest in the local settlement. The shrine grounds cover an area of 8,383 m².Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 196 In ancient times, the shrine grounds spanned approximately 2 chō (≈19,834 m²).Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150 The precincts are covered with moss.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 64 The shrine buildings are constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} style with board roofing, facing south. A single-layered {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|WD=Q3078312|lt=tamagaki|1=Sacred fences}} fence with a gate surrounds the shrine, and a single {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|WD=Q234731|lt=Shinmei torii|1=Torii}} stands at the entrance. == Kano Shrine == Kano Shrine is an auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine of Ise and enshrines a {{ill|Mirror|en|Mirror|WD=Q35197|lt=mirror|1=Mirror}} as its sacred object.Kaneko (1983): p. 59 It is affectionately known as "Kanamori-san." In ''Daijingū Gishiki Kai,'' Nakagawa Tsunemasa explains that "Kanamori" is a corruption of "Kano Mori," and the shrine’s name derives from the name of the area where it is located.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 195 The enshrined deity is Ōkami Mikagekawa no Kami (大神御蔭川神). This deity is considered the god of a tributary of the Sotoda River, which flows through the Kano area, and is revered as a local development god. The same deity is enshrined at {{ill|Mifune Shrine|ja|御船神社 (多気町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mifune Shrine|qq=}}, another auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine. The ''Jinmyōchō Kōshō'' identifies the deity as {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsuhime|ja|瀬織津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 瀬織津姫}} and interprets the name Ōkami Mikagekawa no Kami as "the spirit of {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}, purifying sins by the river." In ancient times, the shrine hall was specified to be 10 shaku (≈3.03 m) in length, 9 shaku (≈2.73 m) in width, and 5 shaku (≈1.52 m) in height, with an altar measuring 4.5 shaku (≈1.36 m) by 2.5 shaku (≈0.758 m) and 1 shaku (≈0.303 m) in height. The shrine was surrounded by a sacred fence (mizugaki), gate, and tamagaki. == Kano Gozen Shrine == Kano Gozen Shrine is Ranked 8th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine, following Kano Shrine. The main hall has been discontinued, and the deity is now enshrined together with Kano Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10 The enshrined deity is Gozen no Kami (御前神). The ''History of Ujiyamada City'' refers to this deity as "Kano Gozen no Kami." Similar to the deity of Kano Shrine, this deity is considered the god of a tributary of the Sotoda River. Gozen no Kami is regarded as the deity who calms and supports Ōkami Mikagekawa no Kami. In ancient times, the shrine had an independent hall measuring 3.5 shaku (≈1.06 m) in length, 2.4 shaku (≈0.727 m) in width, and 7 shaku (≈2.12 m) in height. == History == According to the ''Taijingū Shiki,'' the shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}. It was designated as one of the shrines to be rebuilt every 20 years alongside the main shrine, with officials assigned to oversee the reconstruction. During the {{ill|Middle Ages|en|Middle Ages|WD=Q12554|lt=medieval period|1=Middle Ages}}, the rebuilding process became irregular. For example, during the reconstruction on the 30th day of the 12th month in {{ill|Bun'ei|en|Bun'ei|WD=Q1010103|lt=Bunei|1=Bun'ei}} 3 (Julian calendar: January 26, 1267), the Gozen Shrine, its gate, sacred fence, and torii were not rebuilt. The last clear record of reconstruction was on the 8th day of the 12th month in {{ill|Ryakuō|en|Ryakuō|WD=Q1146182|lt=Ryakuō|1=Ryakuō}} 3 (Julian calendar: December 27, 1340), when materials from {{ill|Kaga Province|en|Kaga Province|WD=Q907469|lt=Kaga Province|1=Kaga Province}} were ordered for the rebuilding. On September 7, {{ill|Kanbun (era)|en|Kanbun (era)|WD=Q1146228|lt=Kanbun|1=Kanbun (era)}} 3 (Gregorian calendar: October 7, 1663), thanks to the efforts of the Chief Priest {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was restored at its current location. A site survey before the restoration confirmed the presence of post holes and decayed pillars, identifying the site as the old shrine grounds. There were no objections to the decision to restore the shrine at this location. The shrine buildings were rebuilt in October {{ill|1916|en|1916|WD=Q2087|lt=1916|1=1916}} (Taishō 5). == Rituals == During the {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai|1=Kinensai}}, {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (in June and December), {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai|1=Kannamesai Festival}}, {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}, and temporary festivals, rituals are performed by priests in front of the shrine. For the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}}, {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}}, National Foundation Day Festival, {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天皇誕生日|1=The Emperor's Birthday|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q72318}}, and Wind Day Prayer Festivals (in May and August), remote rituals are conducted at the Gojōden of the Inner Shrine. == Surroundings == The {{ill|Kumano Saigoku Kaidō|ja|熊野西国街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Saigoku Kaidō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} passes nearby, with remnants of pine tree-lined paths still visible. Additionally, many ancient Kofuns have been discovered in this area, including 19 kofun in the Bōzu-yama Kofun group. * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Tokida Station|en|Tokida Station|WD=Q7813584|lt=Sotoda Station|1=Tokida Station}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|en|Sangū Line|WD=Q872023|lt=Sangū Line|1=Sangū Line}} operated by {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|en|Central Japan Railway Company|WD=Q513679|lt=JR Central|1=Central Japan Railway Company}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』{{ill|Kogakkan University|en|Kogakkan University|WD=Q1797584|lt=Kogakkan University|1=Kogakkan University}}出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[:en:小学館|小学館]]、昭和50th year 4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[東外城田村]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_01.jpg 蚊野神社] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image2 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_02.jpg 蚊野御前神社]}} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0706.html 蚊野神社（かのじんじゃ） 御同座 蚊野御前神社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|蚊野神社|version=77001993|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Kano Shrine (Q17225745)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|ceb|Watarai-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی واتاری|es|Distrito de Watarai|fr|District de Watarai|id|Distrik Watarai, Mie|it|Distretto di Watarai|ko|와타라이군|pl|Powiat Watarai|uk|Повіт Ватарай|zh|度會郡|lt=Watarai district|WD=Q1131032|1=Watarai District}} || {{ill|Kano Shrine|lt=Kano Shrine|ja|蚊野神社|WD=Q17225745|1=Kano Shrine}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} || part of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|WD=Q11581011|1=Kōtai Jingū}} || — || {{coord|34.489433|136.600652|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181116 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17225745}}==Notes== [[da:d:Q17225745]] [[ja:蚊野神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Buildings in Tamaki Town]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tamaki]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]

Kano Gozen Shrine: Gozen no Kami | type = Kano Shrine: {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor Rank), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Tradition holds during Emperor Yūryaku's reign | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = Kanamori-san | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) | location = {{ill|Tamaki|WD=Q587348|1=Tamaki, Mie}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }} '''Kano Shrine''' (蚊野神社, *Kano Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the [[Kōtai Jingū]] (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in {{ill|Tamaki|en|Tamaki|WD=Q1289593|lt=Tamaki|1=Tamaki}}, {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|WD=Q1131032|lt=Watarai District|1=Watarai District}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan. (1975): p. 150 The precincts are covered with moss.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 64 The shrine buildings are constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} style with board roofing, facing south. A single-layered {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|WD=Q3078312|lt=tamagaki|1=Sacred fences}} fence with a gate surrounds the shrine, and a single {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|WD=Q234731|lt=Shinmei torii|1=Torii}} stands at the entrance. == Kano Shrine == Kano Shrine is an auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine of Ise and enshrines a {{ill|Mirror|en|Mirror|WD=Q35197|lt=mirror|1=Mirror}} as its sacred object.Kaneko (1983): p. 59 It is affectionately known as "Kanamori-san." In ''Daijingū Gishiki Kai,'' Nakagawa Tsunemasa explains that "Kanamori" is a corruption of "Kano Mori," and the shrine’s name derives from the name of the area where it is located.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 195 The enshrined deity is Ōkami Mikagekawa no Kami (大神御蔭川神). This deity is considered the god of a tributary of the Sotoda River, which flows through the Kano area, and is revered as a local development god. The same deity is enshrined at {{ill|Mifune Shrine|ja|御船神社 (多気町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mifune Shrine|qq=}}, another auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine. The ''Jinmyōchō Kōshō'' identifies the deity as {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsuhime|ja|瀬織津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 瀬織津姫}} and interprets the name Ōkami Mikagekawa no Kami as "the spirit of {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}, purifying sins by the river." In ancient times, the shrine hall was specified to be 10 shaku (≈3.03 m) in length, 9 shaku (≈2.73 m) in width, and 5 shaku (≈1.52 m) in height, with an altar measuring 4.5 shaku (≈1.36 m) by 2.5 shaku (≈0.758 m) and 1 shaku (≈0.303 m) in height. The shrine was surrounded by a sacred fence (mizugaki), gate, and tamagaki. == Kano Gozen Shrine == Kano Gozen Shrine is Ranked 8th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine, following Kano Shrine. The main hall has been discontinued, and the deity is now enshrined together with Kano Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10 The enshrined deity is Gozen no Kami (御前神). The ''History of Ujiyamada City'' refers to this deity as "Kano Gozen no Kami." Similar to the deity of Kano Shrine, this deity is considered the god of a tributary of the Sotoda River. Gozen no Kami is regarded as the deity who calms and supports Ōkami Mikagekawa no Kami. In ancient times, the shrine had an independent hall measuring 3.5 shaku (≈1.06 m) in length, 2.4 shaku (≈0.727 m) in width, and 7 shaku (≈2.12 m) in height. == History == According to the ''Taijingū Shiki,'' the shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}. It was designated as one of the shrines to be rebuilt every 20 years alongside the main shrine, with officials assigned to oversee the reconstruction. During the {{ill|Middle Ages|en|Middle Ages|WD=Q12554|lt=medieval period|1=Middle Ages}}, the rebuilding process became irregular. For example, during the reconstruction on the 30th day of the 12th month in {{ill|Bun'ei|en|Bun'ei|WD=Q1010103|lt=Bunei|1=Bun'ei}} 3 (Julian calendar: January 26, 1267), the Gozen Shrine, its gate, sacred fence, and torii were not rebuilt. The last clear record of reconstruction was on the 8th day of the 12th month in {{ill|Ryakuō|en|Ryakuō|WD=Q1146182|lt=Ryakuō|1=Ryakuō}} 3 (Julian calendar: December 27, 1340), when materials from {{ill|Kaga Province|en|Kaga Province|WD=Q907469|lt=Kaga Province|1=Kaga Province}} were ordered for the rebuilding. On September 7, {{ill|Kanbun (era)|en|Kanbun (era)|WD=Q1146228|lt=Kanbun|1=Kanbun (era)}} 3 (Gregorian calendar: October 7, 1663), thanks to the efforts of the Chief Priest {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was restored at its current location. A site survey before the restoration confirmed the presence of post holes and decayed pillars, identifying the site as the old shrine grounds. There were no objections to the decision to restore the shrine at this location. The shrine buildings were rebuilt in October {{ill|1916|en|1916|WD=Q2087|lt=1916|1=1916}} (Taishō 5). == Rituals == During the {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai|1=Kinensai}}, {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (in June and December), {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai|1=Kannamesai Festival}}, {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}, and temporary festivals, rituals are performed by priests in front of the shrine. For the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}}, {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}}, National Foundation Day Festival, {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天皇誕生日|1=The Emperor's Birthday|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q72318}}, and Wind Day Prayer Festivals (in May and August), remote rituals are conducted at the Gojōden of the Inner Shrine. == Surroundings == The {{ill|Kumano Saigoku Kaidō|ja|熊野西国街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Saigoku Kaidō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} passes nearby, with remnants of pine tree-lined paths still visible. Additionally, many ancient Kofuns have been discovered in this area, including 19 kofun in the Bōzu-yama Kofun group. * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Tokida Station|en|Tokida Station|WD=Q7813584|lt=Sotoda Station|1=Tokida Station}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|en|Sangū Line|WD=Q872023|lt=Sangū Line|1=Sangū Line}} operated by {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|en|Central Japan Railway Company|WD=Q513679|lt=JR Central|1=Central Japan Railway Company}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』{{ill|Kogakkan University|en|Kogakkan University|WD=Q1797584|lt=Kogakkan University|1=Kogakkan University}}出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[:en:小学館|小学館]]、昭和50th year 4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[東外城田村]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_01.jpg 蚊野神社] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image2 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_02.jpg 蚊野御前神社]}} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0706.html 蚊野神社（かのじんじゃ） 御同座 蚊野御前神社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|蚊野神社|version=77001993|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Kano Shrine (Q17225745)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|ceb|Watarai-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی واتاری|es|Distrito de Watarai|fr|District de Watarai|id|Distrik Watarai, Mie|it|Distretto di Watarai|ko|와타라이군|pl|Powiat Watarai|uk|Повіт Ватарай|zh|度會郡|lt=Watarai district|WD=Q1131032|1=Watarai District}} || {{ill|Kano Shrine|lt=Kano Shrine|ja|蚊野神社|WD=Q17225745|1=Kano Shrine}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} || part of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|WD=Q11581011|1=Kōtai Jingū}} || — || {{coord|34.489433|136.600652|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181116 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17225745}}==Notes== [[da:d:Q17225745]] [[ja:蚊野神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tamaki Town]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tamaki]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | Name = Kano Shrine and Kano Gozen Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Kano-jinja (Naiku) 01.jpg | location = Kano, {{ill|Tamaki|en|Tamaki|WD=Q1289593|lt=Tamaki|1=Tamaki}}, {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|WD=Q1131032|lt=Watarai District|1=Watarai District}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34.489417|N|136.600611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Kano Shrine: Ōkami no Mikagegawa no Kami
Kano Gozen Shrine: Gozen no Kami | type = Kano Shrine: {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor Rank), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Tradition holds during Emperor Yūryaku's reign | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = Kanamori-san | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) | location = {{ill|Tamaki|WD=Q587348|1=Tamaki, Mie}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }} '''Kano Shrine''' (蚊野神社, *Kano Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the [[Kōtai Jingū]] (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in {{ill|Tamaki|en|Tamaki|WD=Q1289593|lt=Tamaki|1=Tamaki}}, {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|WD=Q1131032|lt=Watarai District|1=Watarai District}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan. It holds the 7th Rank among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall, ed. (1929): pp. 9–11. This article also covers '''Kano Gozen Shrine''' (蚊野御前神社, *Kano Gozen Jinja*), which is enshrined alongside Kano Shrine. Both shrines are dedicated to deities protecting the tributaries of the Sotokida River.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 68. == Overview == This section covers information common to both shrines. Located at 1807 Kanosatonaka, {{ill|Tamaki, Mie|en|Tamaki, Mie|WD=Q587348|lt=Tamaki, Mie|1=Tamaki, Mie}}, [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]], {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 60 The area of Kano, where the shrines are situated, lies within a rural landscape, with {{ill|Kano Shrine|ja|蚊野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kano Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Kano Shrine|ja|蚊野神社|lt_ja=蚊野御前神社|lt=Kano Gozen Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kano Shrine|qq=}} enshrined within a large forest in the local settlement. The shrine grounds cover an area of 8,383 m².Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 196 In ancient times, the shrine grounds spanned approximately 2 chō (≈19,834 m²).Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150 The precincts are covered with moss.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 64 The shrine buildings are constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} style with board roofing, facing south. A single-layered {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|WD=Q3078312|lt=tamagaki|1=Sacred fences}} fence with a gate surrounds the shrine, and a single {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|WD=Q234731|lt=Shinmei torii|1=Torii}} stands at the entrance. == Kano Shrine == Kano Shrine is an auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine of Ise and enshrines a {{ill|Mirror|en|Mirror|WD=Q35197|lt=mirror|1=Mirror}} as its sacred object.Kaneko (1983): p. 59 It is affectionately known as "Kanamori-san." In ''Daijingū Gishiki Kai,'' Nakagawa Tsunemasa explains that "Kanamori" is a corruption of "Kano Mori," and the shrine’s name derives from the name of the area where it is located.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 195 The enshrined deity is Ōkami Mikagekawa no Kami (大神御蔭川神). This deity is considered the god of a tributary of the Sotoda River, which flows through the Kano area, and is revered as a local development god. The same deity is enshrined at {{ill|Mifune Shrine|ja|御船神社 (多気町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mifune Shrine|qq=}}, another auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine. The ''Jinmyōchō Kōshō'' identifies the deity as {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsuhime|ja|瀬織津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 瀬織津姫}} and interprets the name Ōkami Mikagekawa no Kami as "the spirit of {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}, purifying sins by the river." In ancient times, the shrine hall was specified to be 10 shaku (≈3.03 m) in length, 9 shaku (≈2.73 m) in width, and 5 shaku (≈1.52 m) in height, with an altar measuring 4.5 shaku (≈1.36 m) by 2.5 shaku (≈0.758 m) and 1 shaku (≈0.303 m) in height. The shrine was surrounded by a sacred fence (mizugaki), gate, and tamagaki. == Kano Gozen Shrine == Kano Gozen Shrine is Ranked 8th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine, following Kano Shrine. The main hall has been discontinued, and the deity is now enshrined together with Kano Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10 The enshrined deity is Gozen no Kami (御前神). The ''History of Ujiyamada City'' refers to this deity as "Kano Gozen no Kami." Similar to the deity of Kano Shrine, this deity is considered the god of a tributary of the Sotoda River. Gozen no Kami is regarded as the deity who calms and supports Ōkami Mikagekawa no Kami. In ancient times, the shrine had an independent hall measuring 3.5 shaku (≈1.06 m) in length, 2.4 shaku (≈0.727 m) in width, and 7 shaku (≈2.12 m) in height. == History == According to the ''Taijingū Shiki,'' the shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}. It was designated as one of the shrines to be rebuilt every 20 years alongside the main shrine, with officials assigned to oversee the reconstruction. During the {{ill|Middle Ages|en|Middle Ages|WD=Q12554|lt=medieval period|1=Middle Ages}}, the rebuilding process became irregular. For example, during the reconstruction on the 30th day of the 12th month in {{ill|Bun'ei|en|Bun'ei|WD=Q1010103|lt=Bunei|1=Bun'ei}} 3 (Julian calendar: January 26, 1267), the Gozen Shrine, its gate, sacred fence, and torii were not rebuilt. The last clear record of reconstruction was on the 8th day of the 12th month in {{ill|Ryakuō|en|Ryakuō|WD=Q1146182|lt=Ryakuō|1=Ryakuō}} 3 (Julian calendar: December 27, 1340), when materials from {{ill|Kaga Province|en|Kaga Province|WD=Q907469|lt=Kaga Province|1=Kaga Province}} were ordered for the rebuilding. On September 7, {{ill|Kanbun (era)|en|Kanbun (era)|WD=Q1146228|lt=Kanbun|1=Kanbun (era)}} 3 (Gregorian calendar: October 7, 1663), thanks to the efforts of the Chief Priest {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was restored at its current location. A site survey before the restoration confirmed the presence of post holes and decayed pillars, identifying the site as the old shrine grounds. There were no objections to the decision to restore the shrine at this location. The shrine buildings were rebuilt in October {{ill|1916|en|1916|WD=Q2087|lt=1916|1=1916}} (Taishō 5). == Rituals == During the {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai|1=Kinensai}}, {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (in June and December), {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai|1=Kannamesai Festival}}, {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}, and temporary festivals, rituals are performed by priests in front of the shrine. For the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}}, {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}}, National Foundation Day Festival, {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天皇誕生日|1=The Emperor's Birthday|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q72318}}, and Wind Day Prayer Festivals (in May and August), remote rituals are conducted at the Gojōden of the Inner Shrine. == Surroundings == The {{ill|Kumano Saigoku Kaidō|ja|熊野西国街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Saigoku Kaidō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} passes nearby, with remnants of pine tree-lined paths still visible. Additionally, many ancient Kofuns have been discovered in this area, including 19 kofun in the Bōzu-yama Kofun group. * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Tokida Station|en|Tokida Station|WD=Q7813584|lt=Sotoda Station|1=Tokida Station}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|en|Sangū Line|WD=Q872023|lt=Sangū Line|1=Sangū Line}} operated by {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|en|Central Japan Railway Company|WD=Q513679|lt=JR Central|1=Central Japan Railway Company}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | Name = Kano Shrine and Kano Gozen Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = File:Kano-jinja (Naiku) 01.jpg | location = Kano, {{ill|Tamaki|en|Tamaki|qid=Q1289593|lt=Tamaki|1=Tamaki}}, {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|qid=Q1131032|lt=Watarai District|1=Watarai District}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34.489417|N|136.600611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Mie | deity = Kano Shrine: Ōkami no Mikagegawa no Kami
Kano Gozen Shrine: Gozen no Kami | type = Kano Shrine: {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor Rank), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) | Established = Tradition holds during Emperor Yūryaku's reign | Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | Other Names = Kanamori-san | festival = | Rituals = [[Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (Harvest Festival) | location = {{ill|Tamaki|qid=Q587348|1=Tamaki, Mie}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }} '''Kano Shrine''' (蚊野神社, *Kano Jinja*) is an auxiliary shrine (*sessha*) of the [[Kōtai Jingū]] (Inner Shrine) of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]], located in {{ill|Tamaki|en|Tamaki|qid=Q1289593|lt=Tamaki|1=Tamaki}}, {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|qid=Q1131032|lt=Watarai District|1=Watarai District}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, Japan. It holds the 7th Rank among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall, ed. (1929): pp. 9–11. This article also covers '''Kano Gozen Shrine''' (蚊野御前神社, *Kano Gozen Jinja*), which is enshrined alongside Kano Shrine. Both shrines are dedicated to deities protecting the tributaries of the Sotokida River.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 68. == Overview == This section covers information common to both shrines. Located at 1807 Kanosatonaka, {{ill|Tamaki, Mie|en|Tamaki, Mie|qid=Q587348|lt=Tamaki, Mie|1=Tamaki, Mie}}, [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District]], {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}.Tatsumi Publishing (2013): p. 60 The area of Kano, where the shrines are situated, lies within a rural landscape, with {{ill|Kano Shrine|ja|蚊野神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kano Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Kano Shrine|ja|蚊野神社|lt_ja=蚊野御前神社|lt=Kano Gozen Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kano Shrine|qq=}} enshrined within a large forest in the local settlement. The shrine grounds cover an area of 8,383 m².Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 196 In ancient times, the shrine grounds spanned approximately 2 chō (≈19,834 m²).Fukuyama et al. (1975): p. 150 The precincts are covered with moss.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p. 64 The shrine buildings are constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} style with board roofing, facing south. A single-layered {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|qid=Q3078312|lt=tamagaki|1=Sacred fences}} fence with a gate surrounds the shrine, and a single {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|qid=Q234731|lt=Shinmei torii|1=Torii}} stands at the entrance. == Kano Shrine == Kano Shrine is an auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine of Ise and enshrines a {{ill|Mirror|en|Mirror|qid=Q35197|lt=mirror|1=Mirror}} as its sacred object.Kaneko (1983): p. 59 It is affectionately known as "Kanamori-san." In ''Daijingū Gishiki Kai,'' Nakagawa Tsunemasa explains that "Kanamori" is a corruption of "Kano Mori," and the shrine’s name derives from the name of the area where it is located.Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 195 The enshrined deity is Ōkami Mikagekawa no Kami (大神御蔭川神). This deity is considered the god of a tributary of the Sotoda River, which flows through the Kano area, and is revered as a local development god. The same deity is enshrined at {{ill|Mifune Shrine|ja|御船神社 (多気町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mifune Shrine|qq=}}, another auxiliary shrine of the Inner Shrine. The ''Jinmyōchō Kōshō'' identifies the deity as {{ill|Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsu-hime|lt=Seoritsuhime|ja|瀬織津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 瀬織津姫}} and interprets the name Ōkami Mikagekawa no Kami as "the spirit of {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}, purifying sins by the river." In ancient times, the shrine hall was specified to be 10 shaku (≈3.03 m) in length, 9 shaku (≈2.73 m) in width, and 5 shaku (≈1.52 m) in height, with an altar measuring 4.5 shaku (≈1.36 m) by 2.5 shaku (≈0.758 m) and 1 shaku (≈0.303 m) in height. The shrine was surrounded by a sacred fence (mizugaki), gate, and tamagaki. == Kano Gozen Shrine == Kano Gozen Shrine is Ranked 8th among the 27 auxiliary shrines of the Inner Shrine, following Kano Shrine. The main hall has been discontinued, and the deity is now enshrined together with Kano Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 10 The enshrined deity is Gozen no Kami (御前神). The ''History of Ujiyamada City'' refers to this deity as "Kano Gozen no Kami." Similar to the deity of Kano Shrine, this deity is considered the god of a tributary of the Sotoda River. Gozen no Kami is regarded as the deity who calms and supports Ōkami Mikagekawa no Kami. In ancient times, the shrine had an independent hall measuring 3.5 shaku (≈1.06 m) in length, 2.4 shaku (≈0.727 m) in width, and 7 shaku (≈2.12 m) in height. == History == According to the ''Taijingū Shiki,'' the shrine was established during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|qid=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}. It was designated as one of the shrines to be rebuilt every 20 years alongside the main shrine, with officials assigned to oversee the reconstruction. During the {{ill|Middle Ages|en|Middle Ages|qid=Q12554|lt=medieval period|1=Middle Ages}}, the rebuilding process became irregular. For example, during the reconstruction on the 30th day of the 12th month in {{ill|Bun'ei|en|Bun'ei|qid=Q1010103|lt=Bunei|1=Bun'ei}} 3 (Julian calendar: January 26, 1267), the Gozen Shrine, its gate, sacred fence, and torii were not rebuilt. The last clear record of reconstruction was on the 8th day of the 12th month in {{ill|Ryakuō|en|Ryakuō|qid=Q1146182|lt=Ryakuō|1=Ryakuō}} 3 (Julian calendar: December 27, 1340), when materials from {{ill|Kaga Province|en|Kaga Province|qid=Q907469|lt=Kaga Province|1=Kaga Province}} were ordered for the rebuilding. On September 7, {{ill|Kanbun (era)|en|Kanbun (era)|qid=Q1146228|lt=Kanbun|1=Kanbun (era)}} 3 (Gregorian calendar: October 7, 1663), thanks to the efforts of the Chief Priest {{ill|Kawabe Masanaga|ja|河邊精長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe Masanaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the shrine was restored at its current location. A site survey before the restoration confirmed the presence of post holes and decayed pillars, identifying the site as the old shrine grounds. There were no objections to the decision to restore the shrine at this location. The shrine buildings were rebuilt in October {{ill|1916|en|1916|qid=Q2087|lt=1916|1=1916}} (Taishō 5). == Rituals == During the {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|qid=Q118906710|lt=Kinensai|1=Kinensai}}, {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (in June and December), {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|qid=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai|1=Kannamesai Festival}}, {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|qid=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}, and temporary festivals, rituals are performed by priests in front of the shrine. For the {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitan-sai|qq=}}, {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genshi-sai|qq=}}, National Foundation Day Festival, {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchō-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天皇誕生日|1=The Emperor's Birthday|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q72318}}, and Wind Day Prayer Festivals (in May and August), remote rituals are conducted at the Gojōden of the Inner Shrine. == Surroundings == The {{ill|Kumano Saigoku Kaidō|ja|熊野西国街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Saigoku Kaidō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} passes nearby, with remnants of pine tree-lined paths still visible. Additionally, many ancient Kofuns have been discovered in this area, including 19 kofun in the Bōzu-yama Kofun group. * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Tokida Station|en|Tokida Station|qid=Q7813584|lt=Sotoda Station|1=Tokida Station}} on the {{ill|Sangū Line|en|Sangū Line|qid=Q872023|lt=Sangū Line|1=Sangū Line}} operated by {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|en|Central Japan Railway Company|qid=Q513679|lt=JR Central|1=Central Japan Railway Company}}. == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 金子延夫『玉城町史 第1巻―南伊勢の歴史と伝承―』三重県郷土資料叢書第61集、三重県郷土史料刊行会、昭和58th year 8月10日、276p. * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』{{ill|Kogakkan University|en|Kogakkan University|qid=Q1797584|lt=Kogakkan University|1=Kogakkan University}}出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * [[福山敏男]]・[[稲垣栄三|稲垣榮三]]・村瀬美樹・胡麻鶴醇之『神宮―第六十回神宮式年遷宮―』[[:en:小学館|小学館]]、昭和50th year 4月20日、246p. * 『日本人なら知っておきたい 伊勢神宮と125の社』タツミムック、[[辰巳出版]]、2013rd year 11月1日、127p. ISBN 978-4-7778-1197-7 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[東外城田村]] == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_01.jpg 蚊野神社] | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image2 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area03/area03_02.jpg 蚊野御前神社]}} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m07/0706.html 蚊野神社（かのじんじゃ） 御同座 蚊野御前神社] - 一般財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|蚊野神社|version=77001993|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Kano Shrine (Q17225745)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Watarai District|en|Watarai District|ja|度会郡|ceb|Watarai-gun|ckb|گەڕەکی واتاری|es|Distrito de Watarai|fr|District de Watarai|id|Distrik Watarai, Mie|it|Distretto di Watarai|ko|와타라이군|pl|Powiat Watarai|uk|Повіт Ватарай|zh|度會郡|lt=Watarai district|qid=Q1131032|1=Watarai District}} || {{ill|Kano Shrine|lt=Kano Shrine|ja|蚊野神社|qid=Q17225745|1=Kano Shrine}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} || part of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|qid=Q11581011|1=Kōtai Jingū}} || — || {{coord|34.489433|136.600652|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181116 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17225745}}==Notes== [[da:d:Q17225745]] [[ja:蚊野神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tamaki Town]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Municipal History of Tamaki]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Tamaki,_Mie]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with 50+ untranslated japanese characters]] [[Category:Pages with 100+ untranslated japanese characters]] [[Category:Pages with 150+ untranslated japanese characters]] [[Category:Pages with 200+ untranslated japanese characters]] [[Category:Pages with 250+ untranslated japanese characters]] [[Category:Pages with 300+ untranslated japanese characters]]
